subject: English
chapter_name: North, South, East, West (The Four Directions)
overall_learning_objectives: Identify and name the four cardinal directions: North, South, East, and West
Understand the relationship between the Sun's position and the directions
Learn the spelling and usage of direction-based vocabulary
Practice using directions to give and follow simple instructions
Develop basic map-reading skills using a compass rose
Recognize how directions help us describe the world around us

lesson_plan.title: Where Does the Sun Rise?
topics_covered: The Sun as our natural guide
teaching_strategy: Interactive demonstration: Using a lamp (Sun) to show where the day begins
The 'Hand Pointing' rule: Facing East to find other directions
Simple mnemonic: 'Eat Sweet Wheat' (East, South, West... almost!)
student_activities: Stand and point: Point your right hand to the 'Sunrise' and identify the directions
Draw a simple sun rising over a hill and label it 'East'

lesson_plan.title: The Compass Friends
topics_covered: North and South: The Top and Bottom of the world
teaching_strategy: Introduce 'Never Eat Soggy Waffles' (North, East, South, West) as a memory tool
Display a large classroom compass on the floor
Explain that North and South are opposites, just like East and West
student_activities: Game: 'Simon Says move North/South/East/West' in the classroom

lesson_plan.title: Mapping the Room
topics_covered: Simple prepositions with directions (e.g., 'To the North of...')
teaching_strategy: Guided walk: Identifying what is on the North wall of the classroom vs. the South wall
Teacher-led description: 'The chalkboard is to the North, the window is to the West'
student_activities: Fill-in-the-blanks: 'My desk is to the ______ of the door'
Small group: Hide a 'treasure' and give 2-step direction clues to find it

lesson_plan.title: Traveling Through the Story
topics_covered: Reading a short story/poem about a traveler
teaching_strategy: Read-aloud: A story of a bird flying South for winter
Discussion: 'Why do animals need to know their directions?'
Final review of the 'Never Eat Soggy Waffles' mnemonic
student_activities: Circle the correct direction in 5 simple sentences
Recite the names of the four directions in a circle

teaching_aids: Lamp or Flashlight (to represent the Sun)
Direction labels for classroom walls (N, S, E, W)
evaluation_methods: Correct labeling of a Compass Rose
Ability to point to directions on command
teacher_tip: Keep it physical! For Class 7 students at an 'Easy' level, directions can feel abstract. Relate everything back to their own bodies. Put 'N, S, E, W' signs on your classroom walls early in the week and keep them up!
